description: Mesoamerican calendar systems — visual structures, cycle names, and generative notes for each tradition.

calendars.name: Tonalpohualli

structure.trecenas: 13 trecenas of 20 days each; each trecena ruled by a patron deity

calendars.visual_format: Wheel or grid; day signs arranged around perimeter; numbers as dot-bar coefficients in center or along inner ring
calendars.generative_hint: Array the 20 day-sign glyphs in a 4×5 or 5×4 grid, or radially around a central axis. Use directional color coding for the four quadrants. Number coefficients as 1–4 dots or 1 bar. Each day sign is roughly square (a glyph block).

calendars.name: Xiuhpohualli
calendars.visual_format: Linear band of 18 month-glyph blocks; often shown as stacked registers in codices
calendars.generative_hint: 18 equal-width panels in a horizontal strip; each panel contains the month glyph (distinctive symbol) and a label. Nemontemi = 5 narrow blank or crossed panels at the end.

calendars.name: Calendar Round
calendars.meaning: 52-year cycle when Tonalpohualli and Xiuhpohualli align

structure.description: The Calendar Round is the interlocking of the 260-day and 365-day cycles. A specific date in both cycles only repeats every 52 years (18,980 days). Called the xiuhmolpilli (year-binding) by the Aztec.
structure.new_fire_ceremony: At end of each 52-year cycle; all fires extinguished; new fire drilled on chest of sacrificial victim atop Huixachtlan hill

calendars.visual_format: Two interlocking gear/wheel diagrams; or a bound bundle of 52 reeds (xiuhmolpilli glyph)
calendars.generative_hint: Two interlocking rings (gears); the inner ring rotates through 260 positions, outer through 365. A coincidence marker at the single overlap point. Alternatively: a simple bound-reed bundle as a single glyph.

calendars.name: Tzolk'in

structure.glyph_style: Each day name is a distinct glyph block; may be shown as head variant (deity head) or geometric abstract sign

calendars.visual_format: Paired columns of glyph blocks; numbers as Maya bar-and-dot system; or wheel diagram; or linear strip
calendars.generative_hint: For Maya style: arrange as two parallel vertical columns of day-sign blocks, each block roughly square with internal geometric design. Numbers as dots (1–4) and bars (5) attached to left of each glyph block.

calendars.name: Haab'
calendars.visual_format: Linear sequence of month glyphs with coefficient numbers; each month glyph is distinct geometric/figurative form
calendars.generative_hint: 19 distinct glyph blocks in sequence; Wayeb' is a narrow/partial block (5 days). Use as a horizontal border band with alternating month glyphs.

calendars.name: Long Count
calendars.meaning: Absolute date count from mythological creation date (4 Ahaw 8 Kumk'u = Aug 11, 3114 BCE)

structure.notation: Five period glyphs stacked vertically; each with a coefficient (Maya dot-bar number)

calendars.visual_format: Vertical stack of 5 glyph blocks; top = Bak'tun, bottom = K'in; each block has a period glyph on the right and a coefficient on the left
calendars.generative_hint: A vertical column of 5 equal-height glyph blocks. Left half of each block = number (dots stacked above bars). Right half = period glyph. Use as a decorative column element in Maya-style compositions. The period glyphs have head-variant forms (deity heads) or abstract geometric forms.

calendars.name: Aztec Sun Stone (Sunstone / Piedra del Sol)
calendars.date: Carved ~1427 CE under Moctezuma I; dedicated 1479
calendars.function: Cosmological monument, not a functional calendar

ring_structure.content: Tonatiuh (sun god) face — tongue extended as sacrificial flint knife, clawed hands holding hearts, surrounded by 4 square cartouches
Four previous suns (Nahui Ocelotl, Nahui Ehecatl, Nahui Quiahuitl, Nahui Atl) in square cartouches at the four cardinal positions; Ollin glyph framing them
20 day sign glyphs in trapezoidal cells arrayed around the circle
Turquoise sky band (xiuhpohualli band) with star-eye symbols and calendar/sky symbols
Ring of jade/precious stone chalchihuitl glyphs (circles with cross)
Sun ray alternating triangles and trapezoidal elements
Two fire serpents (xiuhcoatl) facing each other at bottom; their bodies curl around the entire outer edge; both heads meet at 6 o'clock position

calendars.generative_hint: Concentric ring composition. Each ring has its own motif density. Center face is the most detailed. Rings alternate between dense glyph registers and simpler geometric bands. The outer two serpents create a circular frame. Overall: dark basalt with pigment traces in carved channels.
